heading: Western Area Power Administration borrowing authority
label: 16421a Western Area Power Administration borrowing authority
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Hoover Power Plant Act of 1984, and not as part of the Electricity Modernization Act of 2005, which comprises this subchapter, or the Energy Policy Act of 2005, which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Western Area Power Administration.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 2 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: A
children.text: the Western Area Power Administration may borrow funds from the Treasury; and

children.label: i
children.text: constructing, financing, facilitating, planning, operating, maintaining, or studying construction of new or upgraded electric power transmission lines and related facilities with at least one terminus within the area served by the Western Area Power Administration; and

children.label: ii
children.text: delivering or facilitating the delivery of power generated by renewable energy resources constructed or reasonably expected to be constructed after .February 17, 2009

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 2 Interest
children.text: The rate of interest to be charged in connection with any loan made pursuant to this subsection shall be fixed by the Secretary, taking into consideration market yields on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States of comparable maturities as of the date of the loan.

children.heading: Refinancing
children.label: 3 Refinancing
children.text: The Western Area Power Administration may refinance loans taken pursuant to this section within the Treasury.

children.heading: Participation
children.label: 4 Participation
children.text: The Administrator may permit other entities to participate in the financing, construction and ownership projects financed under this section.

children.heading: Congressional review of disbursement
children.label: 5 Congressional review of disbursement
children.text: Effective upon , the Administrator shall have the authority to have utilized $1,750,000,000 at any one time. If the Administrator seeks to borrow funds above $1,750,000,000, the funds will be disbursed unless there is enacted, within 90 calendar days of the first such request, a joint resolution that rescinds the remainder of the balance of the borrowing authority provided in this section.February 17, 2009

children.heading: Transmission line and related facility projects
children.label: c Transmission line and related facility projects
A
children.text: each other such project; and

children.label: B
children.text: all other Western Area Power Administration power and transmission facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: to pay for any ancillary services that are provided; and

children.label: B
children.text: to meet the costs of operating and maintaining the new project from which the revenues are derived.

children.label: A
children.text: repayment of the associated loan for the project; and

children.label: B
children.text: payment of expenses for ancillary services and operation and maintenance.

children.heading: Limitation on authority
children.label: 4 Limitation on authority
children.text: Nothing in this section confers on the Administrator any additional authority or obligation to provide ancillary services to users of transmission facilities developed under this section.

children.heading: Treatment of certain revenues
children.label: 5 Treatment of certain revenues
children.text: Revenue from ancillary services provided by existing Federal power systems to users of transmission projects funded pursuant to this section shall be treated as revenue to the existing power system that provided the ancillary services.

children.label: A
children.text: the project is in the public interest;

children.label: B
children.text: the project will not adversely impact system reliability or operations, or other statutory obligations; and

children.label: C
children.text: it is reasonable to expect that the proceeds from the project shall be adequate to make repayment of the loan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If, at the end of the useful life of a project, there is a remaining balance owed to the Treasury under this section, the balance shall be forgiven.

children.heading: Unconstructed projects
children.label: B Unconstructed projects
children.text: Funds expended to study projects that are considered pursuant to this section but that are not constructed shall be forgiven.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: C Notification
children.text: The Administrator shall notify the Secretary of such amounts as are to be forgiven under this paragraph.

children.heading: Policies and practices
children.label: 1 Policies and practices
children.text: Prior to requesting any loans under this section, the Administrator shall use a public process to develop practices and policies that implement the authority granted by this section.

children.heading: Requests for interest
children.label: 2 Requests for interest
children.text: In the course of selecting potential projects to be funded under this section, the Administrator shall seek Requests For Interest from entities interested in identifying potential projects through one or more notices published in the Federal Register.
